creator: adridg@freebsd.org
text:
Upstream hasn't got a commit yet for Boost 1.66, so we're (in the most general sense: any project that uses CMake, and Boost, and uses imported targets) kind of stuck: a new Boost will break stuff. Ceph might switch to not using the imported targets, but that means they have to handle all the magic of imported targets by hand.

I think it's time to give CMake upstream a kicking again (or we should just apply the patch "Help find Boost" attached to this PR, as a patch to CMake and bump the portrevision; that would usually involve an exp-run which we should fold into any new Boost exp-run).
